select_when: A strong claim needs an explicit counterclaim before the team can trust the conclusion.
The current reasoning is too one-sided, and progress depends on holding opposing perspectives long enough to produce a better synthesis.
A decision would improve if contradiction were treated as a disciplined input rather than as social friction to be suppressed.
The next useful move is to force thesis, antithesis, and synthesis instead of letting one frame dominate by momentum.
avoid_when: The team is confusing productive dialectic with endless contrarianism and never converting tension into a bounded decision.
The question now needs execution rather than another round of debate, and dialectical rigor would mostly add delay.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on structured thesis-antithesis-synthesis work and anti-one-sided certainty rather than on generic debate language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor discussion-culture sprawl because the raw source is stronger on disciplined synthesis and anti-endless-contrarianism warnings.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans organizational norms, conflict processing, and slow-thinking discipline.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on thesis-antithesis-synthesis, critical self-scrutiny, and structured challenge before judgment.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly grounded in source warnings on endless questioning, contrarianism, and analysis paralysis.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on one-sided arguments that need a counter-position, while the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields a synthesis after disciplined opposition; the typed output is an operational compression.

reasoning_types.note: Dialectical reasoning is strongest as reflective and inferential challenge discipline, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
